inline_comments.message: I think Boolean.FALSE.toString() can be a bit clearer.

I don't think we want to see ALL exception, ALWAYS in the log. For example, input validation errors are self-explanatory and add nothing for troubleshooting a real problem. Especially without seeing the input being validated. On the contrary, they may create unnecessary noise that obscures real problems. I'd recommend writing them with LOGGER.debug(). Please also provide a meaningful message to give the right context - what was happening when the error was written.
Is RandomStringGenerator thread safe? I.e. can the same instance be reused, or do you have to create a new instance every time you need to generate a random string?

IMHO having a dedicated constructor could be a bit nicer

public UnexpectedErrorResponse(String message, String devInfo). 

Especially that UnexpectedErrorResponse can (and should) be immutable just like ErrorResponse.
